mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides an integrated Docker experience on macOS, including a local Kubernetes cluster. It is ideal for developers looking to build and test applications in a Kubernetes environment without the overhead of managing a full cluster. However, it may not be suitable for production workloads due to resource limitations.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step installs Docker Desktop, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> This opens the Docker Desktop download page.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
Run the downloaded installer to install Docker Desktop on your Mac.

```bash

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

sudo cp -R /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil unmount /Volumes/Docker

```

> Replace with the actual downloaded file name if different.
> This copies Docker Desktop to your Applications folder.
> This unmounts the Docker installer.

steps.title: Enable Kubernetes in Docker Desktop
steps.description:
This step configures Docker Desktop to run a local Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

open -a Docker

sleep 10 && open -g 'Docker' --args --enable-kubernetes

```

> This launches Docker Desktop.
> Waits for Docker to start and enables Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
Check that Kubernetes is running correctly within Docker Desktop.

```bash

kubectl cluster-info

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command shows the Kubernetes cluster information.
> This command lists the nodes in the Kubernetes cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This step removes Docker Desktop and all associated Kubernetes resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

sudo rm -rf /Applications/Docker.app

rm -rf ~/Library/Containers/com.docker.docker

```

> This removes the Docker application.
> This removes Docker's container data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Backup your settings, perform the upgrade, and verify the installation.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

open ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

sudo cp -R /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

kubectl get nodes

```

> Download the latest version.
> Open the downloaded upgrade file.
> Replace the existing Docker application.
> Verify that the nodes are still available after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes cluster is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase CPU and memory allocation in Docker Desktop settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl command not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'brew install kubectl'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: connection refused
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes API server is not running.

**Fix:**
Restart Docker Desktop and check Kubernetes settings.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes version mismatch
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop may not be updated to the latest Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Update Docker Desktop to the latest version from the Docker website.

**Affected versions:** <= 4.31

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30, improved resource management.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'Kubernetes master is running at https://192.168.99.100:8443' when running 'kubectl cluster-info'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum system requirements for Docker Desktop.
